title: grindelia camporum var. bracteosa
intro: Grindelia camporum var. bracteosa, also known as gum weed or sticky gumweed, is a variety of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating inflammation and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Grindelia camporum var. bracteosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for external and internal use.
